findings.summary: Every rendered engagement letter prints the firm name twice at the top, once in the letterhead/running header and again as the first body line placed directly above the client's inside address, so the recipient block reads as though the letter were addressed to Pinebrook itself.
findings.evidence: The Dean PLC letter (d:0005) renders as: 'Pinebrook Advisory Group LLC   www.pinebrookadvisory.com' (letterhead) / 'February 7, 2020' / 'Pinebrook Advisory Group LLC' / 'Cynthia Foley, VP Finance' / 'Dean PLC' / 'Re: Pricing Study' / 'Dear Ms. Foley:'. The firm's own name sits at the head of the recipient's inside address, forcing a careful reader to re-read to find the addressee. Identical on all three executed letters (d:0001 Johnson and Sons, d:0008 Reeves, Miller and Thompson); each DocIR opens with a heading block repeating the firm name the letterhead already carries.

findings.summary: The internal working documents carry no date on the document itself: the kickoff-memo header is To/From/Re with the conventional Date line omitted, and the meeting minutes give location and attendees but no meeting date or time; each document's date survives only in its filename.
findings.evidence: Kickoff memo d:0002 header: 'To: Robert Miller, Michelle Lopez. From: Jennifer Fletcher. Re: Kickoff for the operational review, Johnson and Sons.' with no Date line (same in d:0006). Minutes d:0003 open 'A working session on the operational review, held at the offices of Johnson and Sons. Minutes taken by Robert Miller...' and list three attendees but state no date; the meeting date 2019-08-27 appears only in the filename (same for d:0007 and d:0009). The rendered docx add only a 'Pinebrook Advisory Group LLC' running header, no date.
